english: which the finest is obtained. — 2. adj. in compounds: nág-siṅ-ba (nag sing ba) jet-black, very black Thgy.; spró-siṅ-ba (spro sing ba) v. spro (spro).
liquor made of mare's milk, Tartar arrack Sch.
v. seṅ-rás (seng ras) in seṅ-po (seng po).
1. n. of a mountain Glr. — 2. = siṅs-po (sings po).
thin, limpid, of fluids W. (yet cf. séṅ-po (seng po)), *siṅ čug-če* (sing bcug ce) to clarify, to purify.

1. adj. thin, clear W. — 2. sbst. Cs., also čaṅ(-ba)-siṅs (chang (ba) sings) Pth., Lt., *ug-siṅ* (ug sing) Ld. small-beer, the fourth infusion of 'chang', a weak beverage, without any intoxicating qualities, yet not disrelished on that account.
to whistle Sch., sid-sgra (sid sgra) = si-sgra (si sgra).
for सिन्दूर minium, red lead, = li-kri (li khri) Glr.
to evaporate, to soak in, to be imbibed, of fluids, sib-sib (sib sib) or sib-kyis (sib kyis), also sib-kyis tím-pa (sib kyis thim pa) to evaporate quickly.
Lt.; Cs.: a sort of small-pox; Schr.: the measles.
Lex. = bde-ba (bde ba), सुख, sbst. good health, prosperity, vb. to be well, to be well off.
adv. sliding, gliding, slipping, with *čá-če* (ca ce) to move along in this way Ld.

a little piece, a fragment, ro sil-bur (ro sil bur) for the corpse falls to pieces Mil; sil-sil (sil sil) col. id.; *sil čo-če* (sil bco ce) to reduce to small pieces, by breaking, crumbling, plucking etc. W.

(also sil (sil) and sil-sil (sil sil) Lex.) cymbal, lcágs-kyi sil król-ba (lcags kyi sil dkrol ba) Lex., sil snyán ról-mo król-ba (sil snyan rol mo dkrol ba) Glr., Dzl. to strike the cymbals; sil-snyán-ma (sil snyan ma) a female cymbal-player Tar.

1. the tinkling sound of a cymbal, rdza-čáb sil-mas snyán-pa brjod (rdza chab sil mas snyan pa brjod) tunefully flows the brook over its clay-slate bed Mil.; sil-čáb (sil chab) gurgling water, rippling brook Mil. — 2. also sil-dṅúl (sil dngul) the rupee of Ladak, = 4 jau, = ½ of an Indian rupee, *sil-ṅul gyad-di nas* (sil ngul brgyad di nas) a patch of barley worth 8 Silma Ld.

I. pron., also su žig (su zhig), 1. interrog. pron. who? di-na su yod (di na su yod) who is here? kyod su yin (khyod su yin) who are you? su či-skad byá-ba yod (su ci skad bya ba yod) who is it and what is his name? Mil.; which? = gaṅ (gang), rgyál-poi bu su žig (rgyal po'i bu su zhig) which son of the king? Tar., sú-rnams (su rnams), sú-dag (su dag), col. *sú-su* (su su) plural form; in certain popular phrases: *su-gyóg* (su mgyogs) race, running-match W., *su tob gyáb-pa* (su thob rgyab pa) to pounce upon, to snatch away, to plunder C., W. — 2. correlative and indefinite pron.: su mi di ysód-pa dé-la ... sbyín-no (su mi 'di gsod pa de la ... sbyin no) to him that kills this man, I shall give ... Stg.; kyod sṅar sú-la yaṅ ysól-ba ma btáb-bam (khyod sngar su la yang gsol ba ma btab bam) have not you already asked somebody before? Dzl. 73, 14 (acc. to a better reading); su de čáṅ-bai dún-du mčío (su de 'chang ba'i mdun du mchi'o) I shall go to him who has it Do.; su tod-tód (su thod thod) whom it concerns Sch.(?); su yaṅ(ruṅ) (su yang (rung)) whosoever, whoever, any body who, also absolutely: every, every one, all, sú-bas kyaṅ pyís (su bas kyang phyis) later than all (the others) Dzl., when followed by a negative: nobody, frq,; su byuṅ (su byung) Mil. = su yaṅ (su yang); su med kyaṅ (su med kyang) though nobody be present Pth.; su (su), su žig (su zhig), su gaṅ (su gang) is also used for somebody, some one, a certain (but not frq. and more in col. or vulg. language): su gaṅ mdzá-bo žig (su gang mdza' bo zhig) a certain friend; su sér-sna-čan žig (su ser sna can zhig) a (certain) miser; also in the following manner: *su čad dug su tsem dug* (su bcad 'dug su 'tshem 'dug) one cuts out, the other sews W.; *bóṅ-bu su lo-ču-nyi-pa, su lo-čú-pa yin* (bong bu su lo bcu gnyis pa, su lo bcu pa yin) some of the donkeys are twelve, others ten years old. — II. termination: 1. of the term. after a final s, cf. tu (tu), du (du), ru (ru). — 2. of the instr. in Kun.: *'á-pa-su* ('a pa su) from or by the father, inst. of the pas (pas) of B. (cf. सु in the Bräj-dialect of the Hindi). — 3. expletive after nas (nas) Mil. and elsewh. — III. num.: 88.
vulgar corruption of dzo-ki (dzo ki) q. v.
a medicinal root serving as an antidote Cs.
